$comment: STANDALONE walkthrough — Level 4 never existed in 2001 and is deliberately kept OUT of the canon donnie.steps.json run. This slice enters the level directly; on the live site it is reached from the L3 ending, where a near-invisible cellar door appears in the lower-left of the 'time is up, donnie' frame ~108s after the FAA transcript loads. All navigates carry ?seed=42 so the sparrow hotspot — and therefore the screenshots — are deterministic; without a seed the hunt is truly random.

steps.caption: October 2, 1988 — the morning the engine should have fallen. Out of the dark, words assemble: “the tangent universe collapsed. everything you did is undone. and yet — you remember.” A dim red .continue. fades in.
steps.note: diffThreshold tightened: a single assembling word is ~0.1% of the stage — the 0.25% default would treat it as 'no change' and settle mid-assembly.

steps.caption: Click .continue. and the dark gives way to daylight — the Primary Universe, restored.

Roberta Sparrow’s letter assembles, word by word: “a boy came to my door in a dream… i remember a world that no longer happened… every living creature on earth dies alone. — r.s.” As the last word lands, two words ignite red — the way `breathe` once glowed in Donnie’s letter.
steps.note: Fixed, not diff-stable: the letter's last tokens (alone. / — / r.s.) each change <0.1% of the stage, so pixel-diff latches just before the true end. The content is deterministic, so a ceiling is safe; the next step's waitVisibleMs still gates correctness on the seal actually appearing.

steps.caption: The space beneath the signature has become a door. Click it.

Grandma Death’s cellar, in the dark: “find the door. it is not where you left it.” The handle is a faint red mark whose position is RANDOM on every visit — the walkthrough pins it with a fixed seed, a human has to hunt. Find it and click.
steps.note: placeHotspot derives the position from ?seed=42 (mulberry32); without a seed it is Math.random per load. domClick targets the element by selector, so the click lands wherever the seed put it.

steps.caption: A Win98 window blinks awake: WARNING! “you should not have this knowledge.” Close it.
Frank flickers into the second window — “i can see you, donnie.” — above a countdown: 28:06:42:12. “wake up.” Close it to summon the way out.
“>> say the words” glows red in the corner of the frame. Click it.
The screen narrows to a single window and a single question: “the two most beautiful words in the english language.” Karen Pomeroy was right.
Type the words: cellar door.
steps.note: Deliberately does NOT press Enter — the typed words get their own frame; the next step submits.

steps.caption: Press Enter. Say them out loud, if you like — the site can’t hear you, but the door can.
steps.note: Focus is still in the #password input from the previous domType; Enter submits the form (wrong words alert instead of navigating).

steps.caption: A crack of white light widens into an open door, and the final words assemble and hold: “you said the words. the door is open. cellar door.” — THE END.
steps.note: Asserts the true end state against a reference still, like l3-ending. Reference captured from a verified seed=42 run.
